promptingStandard.requiredPromptFields: context role objective instructions steps do dont evidence response_format
promptingStandard.rule: Every workstream execution prompt must include acceptance criteria, explicit out-of-scope constraints, and file paths.
Every prompt must pin: test baseline (1,034+), zero-warning compilation, Axon project path (h:/programing language/axon/).

workstream.name: Compiler Infrastructure Foundation
workstream.scope: Accumulative diagnostics, MIR transform pass architecture, global name interning, stacker integration, granular CLI flags.

deliverables.item: src/diagnostics.rs — accumulative error/warning collector with configurable severity
src/mir/transform/mod.rs — PassManager with ordered pass execution
src/mir/transform/dead_code.rs — dead code elimination pass
src/mir/transform/const_fold.rs — constant folding pass
src/interner.rs — global NameInterner for all identifiers
stacker integration in parser.rs, typeck.rs, mir.rs
CLI flags: --warn, --deny, --allow, -O flags for each pass
Diagnostics wired through all compiler phases in lib.rs

executionPrompt.context: Axon compiler at h:/programing language/axon/. 1,034 tests passing. Current errors are early-exit. MIR is monolithic in src/mir.rs.
executionPrompt.role: You are a compiler infrastructure engineer specializing in diagnostic systems and optimization pass frameworks.
executionPrompt.objective: Build the foundational infrastructure that all subsequent optimization passes will depend on: accumulative diagnostics, modular MIR passes, and global name interning.

instructions.step: Create src/diagnostics.rs with Diagnostics struct (BTreeMap of phase to Vec of Diagnostic), Severity enum (Allow/Warning/Error), DiagnosticConfig with per-category severity, fatal() checkpoint method.
Create src/mir/transform/mod.rs with PassManager that takes a list of Pass trait objects and runs them in order on MirProgram.
Create src/mir/transform/dead_code.rs and src/mir/transform/const_fold.rs as first two passes.
Create src/interner.rs with global NameInterner. Replace String identifiers in AST with interned Name handles.
Add stacker = "0.1" to Cargo.toml. Wrap recursive descent calls in parser.rs and typeck.rs with maybe_grow(32*1024, 1024*1024, || { ... }).
Add CLI flags to src/main.rs via clap: --warn, --deny, --allow, -Odead-code, -Oconst-fold, -Oall.
Wire Diagnostics through lib.rs check_source_full() and all downstream phases.

executionPrompt.do: Write comprehensive unit tests for every new module (min 10 per module). Ensure zero-warning compilation. Run full cargo test after each major change.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not modify existing test assertions. Do not change any existing syntax. Do not break the TypeInterner — NameInterner is separate.
executionPrompt.evidence: cargo test output showing 1,034+ tests pass. New module test counts. Example diagnostic output showing accumulative collection.
executionPrompt.responseFormat: Files created/modified list. Test count delta. Sample diagnostic output.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: All 1,034 existing tests pass with zero regressions.
Diagnostics accumulate errors from multiple phases and display all at once.
--warn/--deny/--allow flags modify diagnostic severity correctly.
PassManager runs dead_code and const_fold passes in order.
Each pass is individually toggleable via -O flags.
NameInterner makes identifier comparison O(1).
Deeply nested input (100+ levels) doesn't stack overflow.
At least 50 new tests across all new modules.

workstream.name: Snapshot Testing Infrastructure
workstream.scope: insta-based snapshot/golden testing for all compiler outputs.

deliverables.item: insta dev dependency added to Cargo.toml
tests/snapshots/ directory with categories: parse/, typecheck/, mir/, llvm_ir/, errors/, fusion/
At least 100 .axon test input files with corresponding snapshots
Snapshot tests for error messages (highest priority)

executionPrompt.role: You are a test infrastructure engineer focused on compiler output regression prevention.
executionPrompt.objective: Establish snapshot testing that catches any unintended change in parser output, type checker output, MIR dumps, LLVM IR, and error messages.
executionPrompt.do: Start with error message snapshots — they are the most user-facing. Then add MIR and LLVM IR snapshots for codegen stability.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not duplicate existing unit tests. Snapshot tests complement, not replace, assertion-based tests.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: insta dependency builds and snapshot workflow functions.
At least 100 snapshot tests exist across all categories.
Error message snapshots cover all error codes (E0100 through E5009).
cargo insta review workflow documented.

workstream.name: Tensor Operation Fusion
workstream.scope: Pattern-matching tensor op chains, fused kernel generation, numerical equivalence verification.

deliverables.item: src/mir/transform/float_combinators.rs — closed subexpression extraction
src/mir/transform/fuse_ops.rs — fusion pattern matcher
src/mir/transform/inline.rs — trivial function inlining
Fused C runtime kernels in src/codegen/runtime.rs
Numerical equivalence test suite
Fusion benchmark suite

executionPrompt.context: MIR transform pass architecture from WS1 is operational. PassManager can run ordered passes.
executionPrompt.role: You are a tensor compiler optimization engineer specializing in operator fusion and kernel generation.
executionPrompt.objective: Implement operator fusion that detects and fuses common tensor operation patterns into single optimized kernels, achieving at least 3x throughput improvement on element-wise chains.

instructions.step: Implement float_combinators pass: walk MIR, identify closed tensor subexpressions (no free non-tensor variables), extract to top-level functions.
Implement fuse_ops pass: pattern-match known fusion patterns (linear+relu, matmul+bias+activation, element-wise chains, reduction chains).
Add fused kernel implementations to runtime.rs: axon_fused_linear_relu, axon_fused_mse, axon_fused_softmax_ce, axon_fused_elementwise_chain.
Implement inline pass for trivial single-use combinators.
Add numerical equivalence tests: run fused and unfused versions on same inputs, verify results within epsilon.
Add criterion benchmarks comparing fused vs unfused throughput.

executionPrompt.do: Test every fusion pattern with edge cases (empty tensors, 1-element tensors, large tensors). Verify correctness before performance.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not fuse across control flow boundaries. Do not fuse operations with side effects. Do not sacrifice numerical accuracy for speed.
executionPrompt.evidence: Benchmark results showing 3x+ throughput. Numerical equivalence test results. cargo test passing.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: linear+relu fusion produces correct output for all input shapes.
Element-wise chain fusion achieves >=3x throughput vs unfused.
Numerical equivalence: all fused ops match unfused within epsilon < 1e-6.
Fusion does not cross control flow boundaries.
-Ofuse-ops flag toggles fusion on/off.
At least 30 new tests for fusion correctness.

workstream.name: Automatic Tensor Parallelism
workstream.scope: MIR-level data dependency analysis, parallel region identification, thread pool dispatch for CPU.

deliverables.item: src/mir/transform/parallelize.rs — DAG analysis and parallel region annotation
Runtime thread pool for parallel tensor dispatch
Parallel scaling benchmarks

executionPrompt.context: MIR transform passes and fusion are operational from WS1 and WS3.
executionPrompt.role: You are a parallel computing engineer specializing in automatic parallelization of data-parallel workloads.
executionPrompt.objective: Implement automatic parallelization of independent tensor operations, achieving linear scaling on multi-core CPUs.

instructions.step: Build data dependency graph from MIR: nodes are tensor operations, edges are data dependencies.
Identify independent subgraphs (no shared edges) that can execute concurrently.
Annotate MIR with parallel region markers.
In codegen, emit thread pool dispatch for parallel regions.
Add runtime thread pool to runtime.rs (lightweight, work-stealing).
Benchmark on standard patterns: parallel matmuls, independent forward passes, data preprocessing.

executionPrompt.do: Ensure correctness first — parallel execution must not change results. Only parallelize pure operations.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not parallelize operations with side effects (print, RNG, file IO). Do not introduce data races.
executionPrompt.evidence: Benchmark results showing >=2x scaling on 4 cores. cargo test passing. No data race warnings.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Independent tensor ops execute concurrently on multi-core CPU.
>=2x scaling demonstrated on 4 cores for independent matmul workloads.
No semantic changes — parallel results identical to sequential.
Side-effecting operations never parallelized.
-Oparallelize flag toggles auto-parallelism on/off.

workstream.name: Fold/Reduce and Language Primitives
workstream.scope: fold/reduce as parallel-aware syntax, tensor comprehensions, list comprehensions.

deliverables.item: fold keyword and syntax: tensor.fold(init, |acc, x| body)
reduce syntax: tensor.reduce(|a, b| a + b)
Tensor comprehension: [expr for x in range]
List comprehension: [expr for x in iter if pred]
Fold/reduce desugar to parallel reduction kernels

executionPrompt.role: You are a language design engineer specializing in functional programming primitives for data-parallel workloads.
executionPrompt.objective: Add fold, reduce, and comprehension syntax that is ergonomic for ML workflows and desugars to efficient parallel operations.
executionPrompt.do: Ensure fold/reduce work on all iterable types (Vec, Tensor, Range). Comprehensions should work for both lists and tensors.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not add bend keyword (conflicts with Axon's imperative model). Do not require immutability for fold/reduce.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: fold/reduce parse, type-check, and produce correct results.
Tensor fold desugars to parallel reduction kernel.
List/tensor comprehensions parse, type-check, and codegen correctly.
Syntax is documented in language tour.
At least 20 new tests for fold, reduce, and comprehensions.

workstream.name: Type System Extensions
workstream.scope: Linear/affine annotations, GPU-optimized ADT layouts, zero-copy verification.

deliverables.item: linear keyword for function parameter annotations
Linearity enforcement in borrow checker
--enum-layout flag for CPU vs GPU ADT representation
Zero-copy verification tests for common tensor patterns

executionPrompt.role: You are a type system engineer specializing in substructural type systems and memory-efficient data representation.
executionPrompt.objective: Add linear type annotations that guarantee zero-copy tensor forwarding, and configurable ADT layouts optimized for different execution targets.
executionPrompt.do: Make linearity opt-in (not required). Provide clear error messages when linearity is violated. Benchmark ADT layouts.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not break existing ownership semantics. Linear is a refinement on top of existing borrow checker, not a replacement.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: linear parameter annotation is enforced — multi-use is a compile error.
At least 5 common tensor patterns verified zero-copy via linearity.
--enum-layout tagged|packed produces different codegen.
ADT layout benchmarks show improvement for GPU-targeted patterns.
All 1,034+ existing tests pass (linearity is opt-in, no impact on existing code).

workstream.name: Language Ergonomics
workstream.scope: Monadic with-blocks, expression-level improvements, documentation updates.

deliverables.item: with-block syntax for effectful pipelines
with/ask desugaring to monadic bind chains
Updated language tour, migration guides, and tutorials
Math-notation block design document (implementation deferred)

executionPrompt.role: You are a language UX engineer focused on making ML code expressive and readable.
executionPrompt.objective: Add ergonomic syntax features that make Axon feel natural to Python ML developers while maintaining compile-time safety.
executionPrompt.do: Ensure all new syntax has clear documentation with before/after examples. Update migration guides.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not add features that conflict with existing syntax. Do not implement math-notation yet (design doc only).

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: with-block parses, type-checks, and desugars correctly.
Documentation updated: language tour, tensors guide, tutorials.
Math-notation design document is complete and reviewed.
All tests pass including new with-block tests.

workstream.name: Advanced and GPU-Deferred Features
workstream.scope: GPU warp divergence optimization, math-notation implementation, shape specialization. Design only until MLIR pipeline exists.

deliverables.item: Design document: GPU branchless transform pass
Design document: math-notation block syntax and parser
Design document: shape-specialized function compilation
Prototype implementations (when GPU codegen is available)

executionPrompt.role: You are a GPU compiler research engineer.
executionPrompt.objective: Produce complete design documents for advanced GPU optimizations that can be implemented once the MLIR pipeline is operational.
executionPrompt.do: Reference XLA, Triton, and TVM architectures. Include performance projections and implementation sketches.
executionPrompt.dont: Do not implement GPU-specific code without a working MLIR backend. Design documents only.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Design documents are complete with architecture, data flow, and pseudocode.
Performance projections include target benchmarks and comparison points.
Implementation is cleanly deferred — no dead code introduced.

phase.name: Infrastructure Foundation
phase.description: Build the foundation: diagnostics, transform passes, snapshot tests, interning, stacker. No new language features yet.
phase.gate: All 1,034 tests pass. Diagnostics accumulate. PassManager operational. 100+ snapshot tests.

phase.name: Core Tensor Optimizations
phase.description: The big features: operator fusion, auto-parallelism, fold/reduce. This is where Axon becomes SOTA.
phase.gate: Fusion 3x throughput. Parallelism 2x on 4 cores. Fold/reduce/comprehensions functional. 200+ new tests.

phase.name: Type System Extensions
phase.description: Linear types and ADT layouts. Refinement on top of working optimizations.
phase.gate: Zero-copy verified on 5+ patterns. ADT benchmarks complete.

phase.name: Language Ergonomics
phase.description: Syntax sugar and documentation. Polish the developer experience.
phase.gate: with-blocks work. Docs updated. Math-notation designed.

phase.name: Advanced GPU
phase.description: Design documents for GPU-specific optimizations. Implementation deferred to MLIR availability.
